rulings.Case Title: GHIGLIOTTI J. JEROME JR VS. VANESSA LINDSAY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notcie Of Motion And Motion To Deem Admitted Requests For Admissions Set 1 And To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, January 28, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 2, CROSS COMPLAINANT VANESSA LINDSAY'S Notcie Of Motion And Motion To Deem Admitted Requests For Admissions Set 1 And To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: No opposition filed. Motion is granted. Sanctions awarded in the amount of $690. No fees are awarded for reviewing and replying to the opposition or appearing at the court hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT A. WASHINGTON VS. SELF STORAGE ADVANTAGE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, January 28, 2013, Line 4, DEFENDANT RANDY MCBRIDE's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant demonstrates with undisputed evidence that he is a good faith purchaser and immune from liability pursuant to California Business and Professions Code § 21711. Defendant has shown with undisputed evidence that he did not enter into any contract with Plainitff or make a representation to Plaintiff concerning the storage units. Defendant has shown that he did not engage in unlawful, unfair, fraudulent or collusive conduct, did not "convert" any of Plaintiff's personal property and was not unjustly enriched at Plaintiff's expense. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: SECTOR 10, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al VS. BANK OF AMERICA CORP. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, January 28, 2013, Line 20, Motion To Compel. Granted. The deposition notice was proper. Plaintiffs may not refuse to produce a witness pending a dispute over separate discovery requests. Plaintiffs are obligated to produce a PMK witness to testify "to the extent of any information known or reasonably available to the deponent." (CCP § 2025.230.) The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: SECTOR 10, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al VS. BANK OF AMERICA CORP. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, January 28, 2013, Line 20, Motion To Compel Production Of Documents. Granted, in part, Plaintiffs' motion to compel as follows: Defendant shall not be required to produce any documents in Response to Request No. 40 seeking floor plans. The minimal relevance of the floor plans is outweighed by Defendant's substantial interest in protecting this information. Instead, Defendant shall produce the following information in response to Request No. 41 with respect to the spaces at issue: (1) Gross square footage; (2) Gross number of exits; (3) Gross number of elevator shafts; (4) Maximum occupancy; and (5) To the extent different from exits, the gross number of evacuation routes. This information shall be designated as "ATTORNEYS' EYES ONLY" under the Protective Order. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: JESSE MORALES et al VS. REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel To Repond To Supp Interogg And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, January 28, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 3, DEFENDANT REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S Motion To Compel To Respond To Supp Interoggatories And For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: No opposition filed. Motion is granted. Sanctions are awarded in the amount of $400 as to Jesse Morales. There will be no attorney time incurred in appearing at the hearing, and the motion with regard to Katherine Morales is virtually identical. Therefore 2.7 hours of attorney time to prepare each virtually identical motion seems excessive.To be clear: The total sanctions awarded are $800--$400 as to Katherine Morales; $400 as to Jesse Morales. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: HBC ASSOCIATES LLC VS. LUCE FORWARD HAMILTON SCRIPPS, LLP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Order Compelling Further Responses To Discovery And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, January 28, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 6, Mtn For Order Compel Further Resp To Discovery Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Special Interrogatory 16. Denied Special Interrogatory 17. Denied in part, granted in part. If Plaintiff is withholding documents on the basis of privilege, a privilege log must be provided. The responses may be subject to further proceedings if Defendant contends that no privilege attaches to any withheld documents. Special Interrogatory 20. Granted. Special Interrogatories 42-49. Denied. These interrogatories address more than a single subject in 12 pgs of declarations. While the declarations are broadly about a single subject, each paragraph presumably deals with a discrete fact. Clement v. Alegre (2009) 177 Cal.App.4th 1277, 1291. Plaintiff may have to respond to additional interrogatories once these declarations are broken down into single subjects. Defendant may well be within its rights to exceed 35 special interrogatories and to require a response to each numbered paragraph in each declaration-provided each of the new interrogatories is brought for a valid purpose and not for the purpose of papering the opposing party. This ruling is without prejudice to Defendant propounding additional interrogatories-each confined to single subjects. The Court makes no ruling as to whether Defendant may propound such additional interrogatories. Form Interrogatories 9.1, 9.2, 17.1, 50.1: Granted. Requests for Admissions 1-5, 7, 9, 11, 12, 15, 17, 21, 22 and 25: Granted. Document requests 15, 22-23, 25: Denied. The Court read, but was not influenced by Defendant's Supplemental Reply Brief. The Court agrees with Defendant that the definition of "Capital Contribution Dispute" is adequately defined for discovery. Plaintiff's Objections to Evidence in the Bales Declaration are overruled. Plaintiff's Objections to the Azarmi Declaration are moot; the Court did not consider the Declaration or its exhibit.No sanctions are awarded to either party. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.

rulings.Case Title: JAMES J NGUYEN et al VS. ANTHONY NGUYEN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs James Nguyen And Madison Nguyen'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Demand
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, January 28, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 5, Plaintiffs James Nguyen And Madison Nguyen's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Demand Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted. Each of the document requests are reasonable and narrowly tailored to elicit relevant evidence. Privacy rights do not seem to be implicated in that many of the responses can be in the form of cancelled checks. The parties may enter into a stipulated protective order, but are not required to do so. If defendants wish to have such a stipulation they must submit a draft to plaintiff's counsel within one week (February 4, 2013). Plaintiff shall have one week (February 11, 2013) to respond. Given the imminent trial date, defendants must produce responsive documents by February 13, 2013 whether or not a stipulated protective order is in effect. Defendants' argument that the complaint itself is time barred is not at issue here in this discovery proceeding. It must be resolved, if at all, by a separate motion or at trial. Sanctions are awarded to plaintiffs in the amount of $1,560. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: AS YOU SOW, A CALIFORNIA NON-PROFIT VS. UNIFIED MARINE INC. DBA SEASENSE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-12-519280 WITH CASE CGC-12-519279
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, January 28, 2013, Line 10, DEFENDANT UNIFIED MARINE INC. DBA SEASENSE's MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-12-519280 WITH CASE CGC-12-519279. The motion to consolidate for all purposes is denied. Complete consolidation is proper only when "the parties are identical and the causes could have been joined." Sanchez v. Suerior. Court (1988) 203 Cal App. 3d 1391,1396. Here, the parties are not identical and moving party did not request a more limited form of consolidation. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: AS YOU SOW, A CALIFORNIA NON-PROFIT VS. OCEAN BIO-CHEM INC DBA STAR BRITE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-12-519279 WITH CASE CGC-12-519280
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, January 28, 2013, Line 11, DEFENDANT OCEAN BIO-CHEM INC DBA STAR BRITE's MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-12-519279 WITH CASE CGC-12-519280. The motion to consolidate for all purposes is denied. Complete consolidation is proper only when "the parties are identical and the causes could have been joined." Sanchez v. Suerior. Court (1988) 203 Cal App. 3d 1391, 1396. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: FELIX LAKS MD VS. SAN FRANCISCO PUBLIC ADMINISTRATOR
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, January 28, 2013, Line 12, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO PUBLIC ADMINISTRATOR's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer is sustained in part with leave to amend and overruled in part. The demurrer on statute of frauds, failure to state facts sufficient to state a cause of action, statute of limitations, and uncertainty grounds is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff is given leave to amend to clarify what causes of action he intends to allege in the complaint and facts showing that the causes of action are not barred by the statute of frauds or statute of limitations. The demurrer to the attorney fees is overruled. This issue should have been raised through a motion to strike, not demurrer, but in any event Plaintiff removed the request for attorney fees and concedes that he is not seeking such fees. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
